title: Environmental Health Program Educational Objectives
content:
Environmental Health Program Educational Objectives

Program Educational Objectives

1. To train technicians who can identify environmental issues such as water, air, soil, and noise pollution, and who are proficient in fundamental concepts and practices of environmental health.

2. To train technicians capable of assessing the effects of environmental factors on human health and planning and implementing measures to minimize these risks.

3. To educate professionals who possess knowledge of national and international environmental laws and standards, and who are equipped to participate in environmental inspection processes.

4. To cultivate technicians who have ethical values and a strong sense of responsibility within the framework of environmental protection, sustainable development, and the protection of public health.
